finding: When options are evaluated separately, a lower-valued alternative is judged more favorably than a higher-valued alternative; this effect reverses under joint evaluation.
An inverse relationship exists between perceived capacity for agency and perceived capacity for patiency; agents are seen as less vulnerable while patients are seen as less capable of action.
A threat to moral self-concept increases desire for physical cleansing, and physical cleansing alleviates distress from unethical behavior.
Ordinary language usage of intentionality terms follows empirically observable patterns that differ from philosophical formulations.
Higher power reduces tendency to adopt others' visual perspectives, accounts for non-privileged information, and accurately infer emotional states.
Physical weight increases judgments of importance and promotes more elaborate cognitive processing.
Thinking about interpersonal-communion traits alters perception of ambient temperature.
Individuals distance themselves psychologically from past experiences with unfavorable self-implications more than those with flattering implications.
Establishing credentials as nonprejudiced licenses expression of attitudes that could be perceived as prejudiced.
Subtle mortality salience increases intergroup bias only when death-related thoughts remain accessible but outside conscious awareness.
Stereotypes are automatically activated, but this activation can be moderated or reversed by explicit counterintentions and available cognitive resources.
Category-congruent information automatically influences judgment even when the category is task-irrelevant and below awareness threshold.
Affirming a positive moral identity licenses subsequent immoral behavior, while threatening moral identity motivates moral behavior to regain self-worth.
Activating superstitious concepts improves performance through increased self-efficacy and task persistence.
Physical warmth increases interpersonal warmth judgments and prosocial choices without explicit awareness of the connection.
Liked or disliked accompaniments directly influence preferences via classical conditioning independent of information processing about product attributes.
Commitment to a relationship promotes forgiveness of betrayal by shifting focus from self-oriented revenge toward relationship-maintenance goals.
Facial muscle activity influences affective responses but not cognitive responses, supporting facial feedback on the emotional dimension.
Grammatical aspect (imperfective versus perfective) influences attribution of intentionality by affecting mental simulation of actions.
A catecholamine reuptake blocker blocks the depletion of regulatory control that normally follows effortful self-regulation.
